key_findings: Phase-1 minimal subset (dns_resolution only) is safe to deploy against 10M+ existing Neo4j nodes — MERGE never raises uniqueness conflicts and is fully idempotent across reruns

ID generation must mirror neo4j_manager.py exactly: sub_+uuid5(NAMESPACE_DNS, subdomain.lower()), ip_+uuid5(NAMESPACE_URL, ip), dom_+uuid5(NAMESPACE_DNS, domain.lower()) — mismatch would create orphaned duplicate nodes

dnsx persistence_gateway.py joins multiple IPs with commas; the sync worker must split on comma and execute one MERGE-5 per IP — treating the joined string as a single IP creates garbage ip_id values that cannot be deduplicated

neo4j_manager.py must NOT be imported — validators dependency causes import failure in this environment (DATABASE_OPS.md Issue-2); ID generation and MERGE logic reproduced inline using only the neo4j driver
MERGE-5 composite pattern (all four MERGE operations in one transaction) is preferred over 4 sequential round-trips — minimizes Bolt connection overhead and ensures atomicity per (subdomain, IP) pair
ConstraintValidationFailed must be classified as ALREADY_SYNCED (not FAILED) — node already written correctly, retrying would always fail and inflate retry_count unnecessarily

decisions.rationale: Neo4j graph writes have higher per-operation overhead than CH INSERT; smaller batches keep Bolt session alive and transaction scopes tight
One Bolt round-trip per (subdomain, IP) pair; 4 separate round-trips would be 4x slower; atomic: no partial write possible

decisions.decision: Split comma-joined IPs from event.value before MERGE
decisions.rationale: persistence_gateway.py dnsx parser: value = ','.join(ips[:5]); must split or generate garbage ip_id from concatenated string
Node already exists with same key — this is a successful idempotent write, not a failure; marking FAILED would cause infinite retry loop

decisions.decision: Use neo4j driver directly, not neo4j_manager.py
decisions.rationale: validators import failure documented in DATABASE_OPS.md Issue-2; ID generation reproduced inline using identical uuid5 logic

decisions.decision: Root domain extraction mirrors neo4j_manager.py multi-part TLD logic
decisions.rationale: Generated domain_id must match existing Domain nodes; different extraction logic would create duplicate Domain nodes with different IDs

decisions.decision: CLAIMED reaper at 600s (identical to CH worker)
decisions.rationale: Prevents stranded batches after worker crash; safe because MERGE is idempotent; consistent with WO-00028 recovery model

worker.cypher_pattern: MERGE-5 composite (single transaction per (subdomain, IP) pair)

error_classes.ALREADY_SYNCED: ConstraintValidationFailed — node exists; mark SYNCED immediately
error_classes.NON_RETRYABLE: AuthError, CypherSyntaxError, InvalidArgument — promote to DLQ immediately
error_classes.RETRYABLE: ServiceUnavailable, TransientError, UNKNOWN — retry with backoff up to MAX_RETRIES

success_metrics.idempotency_validation: count(RESOLVES_TO) identical before and after second sync run

integrity_queries.orphaned_subdomains: MATCH (s:Subdomain) WHERE NOT (s)-[:BELONGS_TO]->() RETURN count(s) AS orphaned_subdomains

risk_model.risk: ID mismatch with existing neo4j_manager.py nodes if uuid5 namespace or case handling differs
risk_model.mitigation: ID generation code explicitly mirrors neo4j_manager.py lines 337-348; multi-part TLD logic mirrored from _extract_root_domain_from_subdomain

risk_model.risk: Comma-joined IP not split — creates garbage ip_id like 'ip_{uuid5("1.2.3.4,5.6.7.8")}'
risk_model.mitigation: map_dns_event_to_params() splits on comma and iterates; verified against persistence_gateway.py parse_event_from_json()

risk_model.risk: validators import failure blocks worker startup if neo4j_manager.py is imported
risk_model.mitigation: Worker imports only from neo4j driver; neo4j_manager.py is never imported

risk_model.risk: Neo4j container not running — all writes fail with ServiceUnavailable
risk_model.mitigation: ServiceUnavailable is RETRYABLE; rows remain PENDING/FAILED with backoff; recovery via DATABASE_OPS.md §Neo4j Recovery; MTTR < 15 min

risk_model.risk: Phase-2 schema changes (Port nodes) cause MERGE-5 to fail if Phase-1 worker is still running
risk_model.mitigation: Phase-2 patterns are additive property SETs and separate MERGE queries; Phase-1 MERGE-5 pattern is unaffected; restart worker with new version

cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00022: sink_outbox table with sink_target column required for batch claim
cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00023: event_sync_ledger, dead_letter_events, sink_outbox_retry_log required for outcome recording and DLQ promotion
cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00025: FM-15 (Neo4j sink) and FM-16 (Neo4j auth failure) drills validate this worker's recovery paths
cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00028: Shared claim pattern (BEGIN IMMEDIATE), CLAIMED TTL, retry taxonomy, DLQ promotion logic
